title: CycleSummary
description: Per-cycle (or per-checkpoint) aggregated aging metrics. One row per (test_id, cycle_or_time_index). Used for aging studies that publish summary metrics (capacity-vs-cycle, R_DC-vs-FEC, etc.) rather than raw cycler timeseries. Stored as rows in harmonized/cycle_summary.parquet.

test_id.description: FK to tests (joins on test_id). Required.

cell_id.description: FK to cells. Denormalized for filter ergonomics (avoids a join to test_metadata for common capacity-fade / R-growth plots). Required.

cycle_number.description: Integer cycle index when the source reports per-cycle data. Nullable because calendar-aging sources and FEC-indexed cycle sources (e.g. Naumann 2021) have no integer cycle count.

equivalent_full_cycles.description: Cumulative throughput expressed as full-equivalent cycles (FEC = cumulative discharge Ah / nominal_capacity_Ah). Standard x-axis for mixed-DoD aging studies. Null for calendar aging.

elapsed_time_s.description: Seconds since begin-of-life (BOL) / test start. Primary x-axis for calendar aging (Naumann storage time). Optional for cycle aging.

capacity_Ah.description: Discharge capacity measured at this checkpoint. Protocol details (CC-only vs CC+CV, C-rate, temperature) belong on test_metadata.

capacity_retention_pct.description: Capacity normalized to BOL, as a percentage (0..150). Convenience field; derivable from capacity_Ah / capacity_Ah[first checkpoint].

resistance_dc_ohm.description: DC pulse resistance. Interpret together with resistance_dc_pulse_duration_s and resistance_dc_soc_pct.

resistance_dc_pulse_duration_s.description: Pulse width used to compute resistance_dc_ohm (e.g. 10.0 for the common R_DC,10s metric).

resistance_dc_soc_pct.description: SOC at which the DC pulse was applied. Naumann-style sources apply the pulse at a fixed checkup SOC.

coulombic_efficiency.description: Discharge Ah / charge Ah for the cycle. Dimensionless, typically 0.95-1.0.

temperature_C_mean.description: Mean cell (or chamber) temperature over the cycle / checkpoint.
